title: Cisco IOS Static Routing
description: Configure static routes using next-hop and exit-interface methods, default routes, floating static routes with administrative distance, and verify the routing table.

tasks.prompt: Enter Global Configuration mode to begin adding static routes.
tasks.explanation: Static routes are configured in global configuration mode. The prompt changes to Branch-R1(config)#.

tasks.prompt: Add a static route to network 192.168.50.0/24 via next-hop 10.1.1.2.
tasks.explanation: A recursive static route points to a remote network and forwards packets to a directly reachable next-hop IP address.

tasks.prompt: Add a static route to 172.16.0.0/16 using Serial0/0/1 as the exit interface.
tasks.explanation: On point-to-point serial links, a static route can reference the outbound interface instead of a next-hop IP.

tasks.prompt: Configure a default static route to the Internet gateway at 203.0.113.1.
tasks.explanation: A default route (0.0.0.0/0) matches all destinations not found in the routing table and is commonly used for Internet exit.

tasks.prompt: Add a backup static route to 10.0.0.0/8 via 10.1.1.3 with administrative distance 250.
tasks.hint: Append the administrative distance at the end: 'ip route [network] [mask] [next-hop] [distance]'
tasks.explanation: A floating static route uses a higher administrative distance so it is installed only when the primary route is unavailable.

tasks.prompt: Add a static route to 10.20.30.0/24 out GigabitEthernet0/0 with next-hop 10.1.1.5.
tasks.hint: Specify both exit interface and next-hop: 'ip route [network] [mask] [interface] [next-hop]'
tasks.explanation: On multi-access networks like Ethernet, specifying both the exit interface and next-hop avoids proxy ARP issues.

tasks.prompt: Remove the static route to 172.16.0.0/16 via Serial0/0/1.
tasks.hint: Prefix the original route command with 'no': 'no ip route ...'
tasks.explanation: The 'no' form of a configuration command removes the matching static route from the running configuration.

tasks.prompt: Exit global configuration mode back to privileged EXEC mode.
tasks.explanation: Verification commands such as 'show ip route' are executed from privileged EXEC mode.

tasks.prompt: Display the full IP routing table.
tasks.explanation: The routing table shows how the router reaches each destination, including codes for static (S), connected (C), and other route sources.

tasks.prompt: Display only static routes in the routing table.
tasks.explanation: Filtering the show command to static routes helps verify your configured entries without connected or dynamic routes.
